reviews.review_title: God game but please add the surface bloc
reviews.review_description: Pls

It needs more tutorials for noobs. I couldn't figure a lot out so I made an in depth guide on the game and yes it include HOW TO STEER A MACHINE! Just copy&paste the following text:


  Besiege VR – Meta Quest 3 Quick Look & Full Building Guide 

 

 

Chapter:1-Introduction 

 

    Besiege VR is a medieval physics-based building simulator, now fully immersive in virtual reality for Meta Quest 3. In VR mode, the core PC mechanics remain, but building, testing, and controlling creations happen in 3D space with your hands. The VR version has a slightly streamlined menu layout for comfort and control, so this guide focuses exclusively on the VR build interface.

 

 

 Chapter:2-Building Interface/Tools 

 

2.1 Build Menu Layout 

 

In VR, the build menu appears as a floating radial or panel UI in front of you, accessible with your controller. You select blocks/tools with a point-and-click motion.

    2.2 Core Tools 

 

 

1. Block Placement Tool

 

Purpose: Place blocks in 3D space on your machine.

 

VR Use: Grab and “snap” blocks into the grid. Rotation is done via wrist twist or joystick rotation.

 

Tip: Hold placement trigger to continuously add in a straight line.

 

 

 

2. Move Tool

 

Purpose: Relocate existing blocks without deleting them.

 

VR Use: Grab a block and drag it to a new snap point.

 

 

 

3. Delete Tool

 

Purpose: Remove blocks.

 

VR Use: Point and click to delete. Hold trigger for multi-delete sweep.

 

 

 

4. Rotate Tool

 

Purpose: Rotate blocks around axes.

 

VR Use: Select the block, then twist your controller or use joystick inputs to rotate.

 

 

 

5. Mirror Tool

 

Purpose: Build symmetrical designs faster.

 

VR Use: Activate symmetry plane; blocks placed on one side appear mirrored on the other.

 

 

 

6. Translate Machine Tool

 

Purpose: Move the entire creation in VR space to reposition it.

 

VR Use: Useful for lining up test runs without rebuilding.

 

 

 

7. Translate Camera Tool

 

Purpose: Move your personal VR viewpoint while building.

 

VR Use: Walk in VR space or use thumbstick locomotion.

 

 

 

8. Undo / Redo

 

Purpose: Reverse/restore recent actions.

 

VR Use: Click undo/redo icons on the VR menu panel.

 

 

 

Chapter 3 – Building Blocks & How to Use Them in VR   

3.1 Structural Blocks

 

Wooden Block – The foundation block. Cheap, light, but breakable.

VR Tip: Build a chassis base before adding functional parts.

 

Brace – Strengthens connections between parts.

VR Tip: Use diagonally to prevent flex in moving machines.

 

Plank / Half Block – Lighter than full blocks for delicate builds.

 

 

3.2 Motion & Steering Parts

 

Hinge – Rotates freely in one axis.

Use: Doors, flaps, deployable parts.

 

Swivel Joint – Rotates in multiple axes freely.

Use: Turrets, swiveling arms.

 

Steering Hinge – Controlled hinge for wheel steering.

Use: Attach to front wheels for vehicle control in VR.

 

Ball Joint – Allows rotation in all directions without detachment.

 

Piston – Extends/retracts when activated.

Use: Lifting arms, pushing mechanisms.

 

 

3.3 Wheels & Locomotion

 

Small Wheel / Large Wheel – Driven via keybinds.

 

Unpowered Wheel – Rolls freely.

 

Tracks – For tank-like movement.

 

Powered Cog – Drives chains/gears.

 

 

3.4 Suspension & Stability

 

Suspension – Absorbs terrain bumps, improving steering control.

 

VR Tip: Use for vehicles with uneven weight distribution.

 

 

3.5 Weapons & Siege Gear

 

Cannon – Fires explosive shot.

 

Flamethrower – Short-range fire blast.

 

Spinning Blades – Melee defense/attack.

 

Grabber – Picks up objects when triggered.

 

 

3.6 Utility Blocks

 

Ballast – Adjustable weight.

 

VR Tip: Move ballast position to balance your machine mid-test.

 

Wing Panel – Adds lift in flying machines.

 

Propeller / Small Propeller – For air vehicles.

 

Vacuum Block – Sucks objects toward it.

 

 

Chapter 4 – Steering Systems in VR 

 

4.1 Basic Front-Wheel Steering

 

Use steering hinges on front wheels only.

 

Map left/right to thumbstick or VR controller tilt.

 

Good for simple cars.

 

 

4.2 Four-Wheel Steering

 

Add steering hinges to both front & rear wheels.

 

Rear steering opposite direction to front = tighter turns.

 

Rear steering same direction = “crab walk” sideways.

 

 

4.3 Tank Steering

 

Use separate powered wheels or tracks on left/right sides.

 

Control each side independently.

 

Push left forward/right backward to spin in place.

 

 

4.4 Articulated Steering

 

Split chassis with a pivot hinge in the middle.

 

Rotate hinge for steering instead of wheels.

 

Great for heavy machinery.

 

 

Chapter 5 – Advanced VR Tips & Hidden Tricks   

1. Invisible Braces – Build braces, then remove visual mesh in settings for cleaner look.

 

 

2. Hidden Weapon Bays – Hide weapons inside hinged panels for surprise attacks.

 

 

3. Weight Shifting for Climbing – Move ballast during play to tip vehicle for climbing.

 

 

4. Snap-Free Mode – Disable snap for precise custom angles in VR.

 

 

5. Miniaturized Gears – Small cog + big cog for torque boost.

 

 

6. VR Scaling – In VR you can “lean in” close to build micro-details impossible on flat screen.

 

 

7. Multi-Layer Steering – Combine tank steering with hinge steering for extreme control.

reviews.review_title: Wanted this game for a while
reviews.review_description: Could not figure out what to do. Game looked great and I have played most games by the devs but honestly couldn't figure out what to to to get the machine to work so uninstalled it.

developer_response.body:
Hi, don't quit right away, write us at 3r.games/Discord and we'll help you! This game is really deep if you want to dive in but can be also crazy fun when you're just enjoying the basics. :)

Regards 
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description: I already loved this game from pc. Transition to vr is great. I think I actually find the building quite a bit easier than on PC. Controlling a complex machine can be a bit worse as less buttons at your disposal but has been handled pretty well with two set available for each controller. Would like a little more explanation of controls at start, struggled to find some such as delete a part, but got there in the end. Loads of fun. Reasonable length of gameplay. Probably 4-8hrs if trying to go straight through without using built in machines.

Besiege VR is an incredibly immersive and visually stunning experience that brings engineering creativity to life like never before. Although I haven’t played the original PC version, the VR adaptation stands strong on its own, offering both accessibility and depth.

One of the most impressive aspects is the sheer immersion. Being able to view your contraption from the inside during "destruction" phase truly elevates the gameplay. Whether you're riding along with a siege engine or adjusting parts, the sense of scale and presence is remarkable.

The game strikes a perfect balance between simplicity and complexity. It can be as straightforward as slapping wheels on a block and sending it off with basic controls — or as intricate as building multi-functional machines with advanced controls, hinges, and logic.

The creative possibilities feel endless. With intuitive VR controls (after getting used to them) and a robust physics system, Besiege VR invites you to experiment, fail spectacularly, and improve iteratively — which is part of the fun.

Verdict:
An outstanding VR title that combines creative freedom with hands-on engineering in the most immersive way possible. Highly recommended.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much! :)

Regards
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_title: Fun but camera control makes me sick
reviews.review_description: I enjoy besieged in PC years ago and I think it's a great game, but I can't play it in VR with the smooth camera controls. I get motion sickness, I wish I could pick up the machines and twist them in my hand to build. Maybe if I had a bigger space and could just walk around the machine/level

developer_response.body:
In building phase you can jump around your machine by double tapping B button, it's faster and you don't need to fly around. In battle phase make sure to check the camera settings, you don't need to use camera follow feature. Please try these and reconsider your rating, this isn't a 3 star game for sure! :D

Regards
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description: This is beyond my simple comprehension and logic as a mere mortal, we do not deserve such a gift. Take my lamp as a sacrifice for this.
This game for me is a good challenge, I like building things so, building things to make other things go bye bye makes me a happy guy 😁

Good fun, good physics and its nice creating a new machine to meet the next level's challenges.   It feels good to create something that works but also fun when it fails catastrophically.  

I enjoy the excessive blood spalts when one of your machines chops up an enemy soldier (although that can be turned off if you dont like that sort of thing).

developer_response.body:
Thanks! 😊

Regards 
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description:
This perfectly blends creativity, destruction, and engineering into an immersive sandbox game that’s both fun and challenging. Building medieval siege machines with full VR interaction is fun fun fun — and watching them wreak havoc is deeply satisfying!

The controls are smooth, and the variety of parts lets your imagination run wild. Whether you're designing intricate contraptions or just experimenting with chaos, the game rewards both strategy and playfulness. 

I did struggle to get to understand it at first, as from the start you can build whacky and weird contraptions, for me I would have liked a gentle tutorial bringing in more and more stuff as the game levels progress.  However, that said, it is highly recommended for anyone who loves building games, physics-based challenges, or just smashing stuff in the most creative way possible!

developer_response.body:
Woah, thank you very much for this detailed opinion. Also: make sure to NOT close the tutorials right away, they're there for a reason! :D

Regards
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description: This sandbox game is useless without actual multiplayer like the steam version

Hi, don't downrate the game we've remade from scratch for VR and especially for not having features that were never intended for this version. This definitely isn't a 1 star game. Cheers!

Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description:
Only a few hours in, and Besiege VR has already hooked me. Building medieval siege engines in full VR is a brilliant mix of creativity and chaos, it really gets you thinking. The physics feel great, and watching your contraptions wobble, collapse, or somehow succeed is all part of the fun.

I do wish the tutorial went a bit deeper, as there’s a lot to learn. But honestly, the trial-and-error process is half the charm. If you ever loved tinkering with Lego or Meccano, this will feel like a nostalgic fever dream, with explosions.

This is a fun game to pass some time in, it’s pretty quick to build a basic machine for the task at hand or you can spend ages creating something that is over-engineered and destined to tear itself apart. VR controls work well and you get a nice sense of scale in game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you! :)

Regards
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description:
Medieval and machines of destruction, with a sandbox… what more could you want!?

Given the freedom to do as much or as little as you want, when it comes to building your death machines!

I did find it a bit confusing and took me a while to get used to.
Took me a while to work out how to change into simulation mode (button on your left wrist).
And I couldn’t get the follow vehicle to work which is on the right wrist.

There isn’t much instruction with what the bits do, you have to experiment and learn as you go along.

I like the physics in the game, things getting blown off all over the place!

But it could do with a level reset.. if there is one, I couldn’t find it.

But once you get used to the game and how it works, it is great fun letting the creativity and engineer in yourself, run wild! 

Graphics are ok, animations are a bit basic… but the game itself is fun and unique.

*Update.. I have changed my score to 5 stars, as realised the issues were my fault

developer_response.body:
Hi, start again from level 1 but don't close tutorials right away, they'll provide you with proper onboarding! :) In case you forgot something you can always open game menu, and go to settings for all the tutorials in one place. To control the machine - start the simulation, hold right grip and use right stick, all these infos are in the tutorials. In case you have any questions feel free to join us at 3r.games/Discord and if 4 stars mean you couldn't find tutorials... Well, they are there! Cheers from our team! :)

Regards 
Piotr, 3R Games CEO

reviews.review_description: The VR game is stunning, I recommend it, but only if you feel nostalgic for the PC version.  4 ✨ control are not easy

If you want refund you need to process it with Meta, it's beyond us devs. I'd encourage you to spend some more time with the game though, we've tested and polished it with many, many players and it should be easier to get once you try it a few times more. Cheers! :)

Regards
Piotr, 3R Games CEO
